name: wire_integration_to_agent
description: Wire a third-party integration's tools into one agent. Call this AFTER the user OAuths the app via `start_integration_connect` and confirms the connection appears in `list_sources`. Args: `agent_slug` (the agent to wire); `app_slug` (the integration, e.g. 'slack', 'gmail'). The structured response is shaped: `{wired: bool, mode: 'explicit'|'allow_all'|'ingestion_only'|'not_connected'|'unknown_provider', sources_added: list, tool_count: int, message: str}`. Branch on `wired` + `mode` — `wired=true, mode='explicit'` means the agent now has tool_count tools; `wired=true, mode='allow_all'` means the agent inherits an unfiltered allowlist for the wired source(s); `wired=false, mode='not_connected'` means tell the user to OAuth first; `wired=false, mode='ingestion_only'` means the app feeds the graph but exposes no tools (use discover/interpret/search instead). Surface `message` to the user verbatim.

app_slug.description: Integration app slug, e.g. 'slack', 'gmail', 'linear'. Must match the slug surfaced by `list_apps` for an active OAuth-connected integration.
